sections.paragraph:
f.-

(1) cassia, Gr. κασία (Laurus Cassia, Linn.), a bark similar to cinnamon, but less aromatic, so called from its being peeled off (root קָצַע), pl. קְצִיעֹות Psa 45:9 see Celsii Hierobot., t. ii. p. 360. Arab. قَصِيغَةُ id., Cast.

(2) [Kezia], pr.n. of a daughter of Job, Job 42:14.
